A Agents
AI Engineer Backend Architect Brand Guardian Content Creator DevOps Automator Frontend Developer Growth Hacker Mobile App Builder Project Shipper Rapid Prototyper Sprint Prioritizer UI Designer UX Researcher Visual Storyteller Whimsy Injector

C3 Services MCP API

Per-service API discovery, proxy calls, and lifecycle management

Service Discovery (7 tools)

MCPservice_list_apis

List all services that expose APIs, with their base URLs and auth requirements.

MCPservice_get_info

Get detailed info about a specific service — domain, VM, port, availability, description.

string — Service name (e.g. matomo, vaultwarden, ntfy)
MCPservice_get_spec

Get the API specification for a service — endpoint groups, auth config, available operations.

string — Service name
MCPservice_discover_all

Auto-discover all active services across all VMs. Returns service name, URL, status, and API availability.

MCPservice_api_call

Proxy an API call to any service through the C3 API gateway. Handles auth injection and response formatting.

string — Target service name
string — HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
string — API path (e.g. /api/v1/users)
object? — Request body for POST/PUT
object? — Query parameters
MCPget_service_detail

Get full service detail from config.json — VM assignment, ports, volumes, dependencies.

string — Service name
MCPlist_services

List all declared services from infrastructure config with VM, port, and availability.

Health & Monitoring (11 tools)

MCPhealth_alive

Quick ping check — is the service responding? Returns HTTP status code and latency.

string — Service name
MCPhealth_declared

Check if a service is declared in the infrastructure config (should exist).

string — Service name
MCPhealth_deployed

Check if a service is deployed (container running on target VM).

string — Service name
MCPhealth_drift

Detect config drift between declared state (source) and actual state (deployed).

string — Service name
MCPhealth_status

Full health dashboard — combines alive, declared, deployed, and drift checks for all services.

MCPhealth_tier1

Tier 1 health check — infrastructure layer (VMs reachable, WireGuard up, DNS resolving).

MCPhealth_tier2

Tier 2 health check — platform layer (Docker running, containers up, ports listening).

MCPhealth_tier3

Tier 3 health check — application layer (service health endpoints, API responses, auth working).

MCPprofile_container

Profile a container — CPU, memory, network I/O, disk usage, restart count.

string — Container name
string — VM alias
MCPprofile_vm

Profile a VM — CPU load, memory, disk, swap, uptime, running containers.

string — VM alias (e.g. gcp-proxy, oci-apps)
MCPcheck_vm

Quick VM connectivity check via SSH. Returns reachable status and basic system info.

string — VM alias

Lifecycle Management (8 tools)

MCPvm_start

Start a stopped VM via cloud provider API (OCI/GCP).

string — VM alias
MCPvm_stop

Stop a running VM gracefully.

string — VM alias
MCPvm_reset

Hard reset a VM (force restart). Use when VM is unresponsive.

string — VM alias
MCPcontainer_start

Start a stopped Docker container on a VM.

string — Container name
string — VM alias
MCPcontainer_stop

Stop a running container gracefully.

string — Container name
string — VM alias
MCPcontainer_restart

Restart a container (stop + start).

string — Container name
string — VM alias
MCPservice_start

Start a service (docker compose up). Handles wake-on-demand services.

string — Service name
MCPservice_stop

Stop a service (docker compose down).

string — Service name

Infrastructure (6 tools)

MCPlist_vms

List all VMs from infrastructure config with alias, IP, WireGuard IP, RAM, and assigned services.

MCPc3_topology

Full infrastructure topology — VMs, services, networking, traffic flow (Cloudflare → Caddy → WireGuard → VM).

MCPc3_topology_drift

Compare declared topology vs actual state. Detects missing containers, wrong ports, stale configs.

MCPc3_topology_security

Security audit of infrastructure topology — exposed ports, missing auth, TLS status, firewall rules.

MCPc3_vm_status

Detailed VM status — cloud provider state, SSH reachability, resource usage, running containers.

string — VM alias
MCPc3_report

Generate a comprehensive infrastructure report — health, drift, security, resource usage across all VMs.

Build & Deploy (6 tools)

MCPbuild_service

Run build.sh build for a cloud service — nix build + secrets to dist/.

string — Service directory name (e.g. bb-sec_caddy)
MCPbuild_all

Build all cloud services. Runs build.sh build in each service directory.

MCPbuild_ship

Full deploy pipeline: build + secrets + deploy + compose. Ships a service to its target VM.

string — Service directory name
MCPbuild_docker

Build a Docker image for a service (used for REMOTE_BUILD services).

string — Service directory name
boolean? — Build on target VM instead of locally
MCPsecrets_status

Check SOPS secrets status for all services — encrypted, decrypted, stale, or missing.

MCPbackup_trigger

Trigger a backup for a service's persistent data volumes.

string — Service name

Docker Operations (4 tools)

MCPdocker_ps

List Docker containers on a VM with status, image, ports, and uptime.

string — VM alias
boolean? — Include stopped containers
MCPdocker_logs

Get container logs via SSH. Read-only inspection.

string — Container name
string — VM alias
number? — Number of tail lines (default: 100)
MCPdocker_control

Control a Docker container — start, stop, restart, or remove.

string — Action: start, stop, restart, rm
string — Container name
string — VM alias
MCPdocker_compose_up

Run docker compose up -d in a service's deploy directory on a VM.

string — Service name
string — VM alias
